title: onopordum nervosum subsp. castellanum
intro: Onopordum nervosum subsp. castellanum, also known as Castilian cotton thistle, is a plant species in the sunflower family, characterized by its tall stems and spiny leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are believed to help treat various health issues such as wounds, fever, and digestive problems. The health benefits of Castilian cotton thistle are attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. Traditional herbal preparations of Castilian cotton thistle include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
